heading.es: Por qué importan las marcas de los pernos
heading.fr: Pourquoi le marquage des boulons est essentiel
heading.pt: Por que as marcações de parafusos são importantes
heading.ja: ボルトの識別マークが重要な理由

body.en: Bolt markings provide critical information about the fastener's strength, material, and performance characteristics. Using the wrong grade bolt can lead to joint failure, equipment damage, and safety hazards. Understanding markings ensures you select the correct fastener for your application, preventing costly failures and safety incidents in African construction and industrial projects. Related resources: high-tensile bolts anchor bolts stainless steel fasteners hex bolts lock nuts washers self-drilling screws socket screws. This procurement framework applies to fastener sourcing for construction, mining, energy, automotive, and general industrial applications. The decision logic prioritizes quality consistency, supplier reliability, and total cost of ownership over short-term unit price. For B2B buyers in Africa, Latin America, and Southeast Asia, additional considerations include regional customs requirements, container consolidation options, and payment security mechanisms. Standardized specifications that travel across projects reduce training time, RFQ processing time, and supplier onboarding effort. heading.es: Marcas de grado de pernos métricos
heading.fr: Marquage des classes de boulons métriques
heading.pt: Marcações de classe de parafusos métricos
heading.ja: メートル規格ボルトの強度区分マーク

body.en: Metric bolts use a two-number grading system (e.g., 8.8, 10.9, 12.9). The first number represents 1/100 of the nominal tensile strength in N/mm². The second number is 10 times the ratio of proof stress to tensile strength. Higher numbers indicate stronger bolts. Standards-based procurement requires continuous learning as ISO, ASTM, DIN, and GB standards are revised every 3-5 years. Subscribe to standards update notifications and review specifications annually. The most common standards used in international fastener trade are ISO 898-1 (mechanical properties), ISO 3506-1 (stainless), ISO 3269 (acceptance inspection), ISO 4042 (coatings), and ISO 1502 (thread gauges). For B2B buyers, building a standards reference library with at least the top 20 standards relevant to your product mix is essential. heading.es: Cómo leer las marcas de los pernos
heading.fr: Comment lire les marquages des boulons
heading.pt: Como ler as marcações de parafusos
heading.ja: ボルトマークの読み方

body.en: Look for the grade marking on the bolt head. For metric bolts, the marking will show the two numbers separated by a decimal (e.g., 8.8). The marking may also include the manufacturer's trademark. For hex bolts, look for the marking on the top of the head. For socket head capscrews, the grade is typically marked on the side. heading.fr: Marquage des boulons en acier inoxydable
heading.pt: Marcações de parafusos de aço inoxidável
heading.ja: ステンレス製ボルトの識別マーク

body.en: Stainless steel bolts use a different marking system. Look for A2 or A4 (property class) and the steel grade (e.g., 304, 316). A2 (304) is the most common stainless steel for general use. A4 (316) offers superior corrosion resistance for coastal and marine environments. Additional markings may indicate if the bolt is austenitic (A) or martensitic (C). The verification checklist applies to both initial supplier qualification and ongoing batch inspection. For high-risk applications (load-bearing structural, pressure vessels, mining, marine), add fatigue testing, salt-spray testing (ASTM B117), torque-tension testing, and post-installation inspection. heading.fr: Reconnaissance des marquages dans les projets africains

body.en: When sourcing fasteners for African projects, verify markings carefully: always confirm grade matches your specifications; be wary of counterfeit bolts with incorrect markings; for structural applications, insist on third-party test certificates; and store bolts by grade to prevent mixing. African construction projects have seen failures from counterfeit fasteners—always verify markings and source from reputable suppliers. Supplier evaluation should be a continuous process, not a one-time exercise. Re-evaluate suppliers annually based on delivery performance, quality metrics, price competitiveness, and responsiveness. Maintain a supplier scorecard with weighted metrics: on-time delivery (25%), quality acceptance rate (30%), price competitiveness (20%), responsiveness (15%), and documentation accuracy (10%). Drop suppliers scoring below 70% over a 6-month period and qualify replacements. Supplier evaluation should be a continuous process, not a one-time exercise. faqItems.question: How do I read bolt grade markings?
faqItems.answer: Metric bolt grades: Number on head = 1/100 of tensile strength in MPa. Example: 8.8 = 800 MPa tensile, 640 MPa yield. Two numbers: First (8) = tensile/100; Second (8) = yield ratio × 10. Imperial bolts: Grade 5 = 120,000 PSI; Grade 8 = 150,000 PSI. Look for manufacturer logo + grade marking on bolt head.

faqItems.question: What do the numbers on bolt heads mean?
faqItems.answer: Metric markings: 4.6, 8.8, 10.9, 12.9 are most common. First number ÷ 100 = tensile strength in MPa. Second number ÷ 10 = yield ratio (percentage of tensile before yielding). Imperial: Grade 2 (low), Grade 5 (medium), Grade 8 (high). Always match exact grade specified - mixing grades causes structural failure.

faqItems.question: How to identify metric vs imperial bolt grades?
faqItems.answer: Metric bolts: Head shows class (8.8, 10.9, etc.) with manufacturer logo. Thread pitch is fine (1.0-1.5mm for M10). Imperial bolts: Head shows radial lines (Grade 5 = 3 lines at 120° intervals). Thread pitch is coarser (1.5-2.0mm for 3/8 inch). Measure with caliper: M10 = 10mm diameter; 3/8 inch = 9.525mm diameter.

faqItems.question: What is the tensile strength of Grade 8.8 bolts?
faqItems.answer: Grade 8.8 metric bolt: Tensile strength = 800 MPa (116,000 PSI); Yield strength = 640 MPa (92,800 PSI); Proof load = 580 MPa. This is intermediate strength - suitable for most construction and machinery applications. For critical structural work, specify Grade 10.9 (1000 MPa tensile) or Grade 12.9 (1200 MPa tensile).

faqItems.question: Why do bolt markings matter for structural applications?
faqItems.answer: Bolt markings ensure correct strength for load requirements. Using wrong grade causes: Under-specification = structural failure, collapse risk; Over-specification = cost waste, fitting problems. Critical factors: 1) Tensile strength (load capacity); 2) Yield strength (permanent deformation point); 3) Proof load (maximum before plastic deformation). Always verify markings match specification on engineering drawings.
